name: Democratic Republic of the Congo

visa_free.notes: Reciprocal visa waiver effective September 1, 2023. DRC lifted visa restrictions for Kenyan nationals on the same date that Kenya waived requirements for Congolese nationals (effective August 25, 2023). Announced by the DRC Directorate General of Migration (DGM). Maximum stay of 30 days confirmed on the Kenya side of the reciprocal agreement. Purpose: tourism and business.

DRC officially waived visa requirements for Tanzanian citizens effective March 20, 2025. Reciprocal arrangement — Tanzania had waived visa fees for Congolese travelers effective September 2, 2023. Announced by Tanzania's Ministry of Foreign Affairs and East African Cooperation. Maximum stay not officially specified from a DRC official source. Part of EAC Common Market Protocol implementation.

Reciprocal visa waiver agreed during 8th Ordinary Session of the Joint Permanent Commission between Uganda and DRC on October 21, 2023, in Kinshasa. Uganda lifted visa requirements for DRC nationals effective January 1, 2024. The reciprocal DRC waiver for Ugandans was agreed in the same declaration. Maximum stay not specified in the official EAC press release. Implementation consistency is uncertain — some aggregator sources indicate Ugandans may still be required to obtain a visa in practice; confirmed direction is Uganda admits DRC nationals, but full confirmation of DRC admitting Ugandans without visa from an official DRC domain was not found.

e_visa.nationality: All eligible nationalities (nationals of countries with no DRC diplomatic representation, or any foreigner travelling for business or tourism)
e_visa.notes: Three types of e-visa (visa volant) are offered: ordinary flying visa (visa volant ordinaire), specific flying visa (visa volant spécifique), and family visit flying visa (visa volant pour visite de famille). Eligible applicants per FAQ: (A) nationals of countries without DRC diplomatic representation; (B) foreigners wishing to visit for business; (C) foreigners wishing to visit for tourism. Total e-visa cost: USD 300 (USD 50 non-refundable admin fees + USD 250 visa fee). Processing time: 72 hours. Upon arrival, the e-visa holder receives a VAP (Visa Aéroportuaire / airport visa) valid for 7 days; the VAP costs USD 90 payable at the border entry point. The e-visa is valid for use within 3 months of issue date. Single entry. Applicable at any DRC border post (air, land, river/lake).

visa_policy.notes: DRC requires a visa for entry for most foreign nationals. The official e-visa portal evisa.gouv.cd (operated by the Direction Générale de Migration under the .gouv.cd domain) confirms the flying visa (e-visa) system and its fees. The official DGM website (dgm.cd) does not publish a public list of visa-exempt or visa-on-arrival nationalities. As of 2023–2025, DRC has entered into reciprocal visa-free arrangements with EAC partner states Kenya (effective September 1, 2023), Tanzania (effective March 20, 2025), and Uganda (agreed October 21, 2023; Uganda side effective January 1, 2024). These waivers were confirmed through EAC official press releases. US citizens: the DRC Embassy in Washington DC (ambadrcusa.org) notes that DRC and USA signed a visa reciprocity agreement; a 2-year visa for US citizens costs USD 185. Visa types at embassies: airport transit (USD 45 one-way / USD 75 roundtrip), single entry (USD 100–400 depending on validity), multiple entry (USD 120–450 depending on validity).

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official DRC government source. Not advertised on evisa.gouv.cd, dgm.cd, or ambadrcusa.org.

programs.name: Establishment Visa (Visa d'Etablissement)
programs.notes: Issued to foreigners wishing to settle in the DRC. Grants resident status and authorises professional or economic activities under conditions set by law. Subtypes: Regular, Professional, Studies, Matrimonial, Permanent. For foreign workers, initial temporary work visa is limited to 6 consecutive months; after approval, may qualify for establishment visa with minimum 1-year validity. No minimum investment amount stated on official sources for the standard establishment visa. ANAPI (National Agency for Investment Promotion) provides facilitation services for initial investments over USD 200,000.

agreements: EAC (East African Community) — DRC member state since July 2022; EAC Common Market Protocol requires visa-free movement among partner states
SADC (Southern African Development Community) — DRC is a member state per sadc.int
COMESA (Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa) — DRC is a member state per comesa.int

conditional_access.conditions: Agreement between the Government of the Russian Federation and the Government of the Democratic Republic of the Congo on visa-free travel for holders of diplomatic and service passports, signed 27 July 2023 on the sidelines of the second Russia-Africa Summit in St. Petersburg. Citizens of either state holding a valid diplomatic or service passport may enter, leave, transit and stay in the territory of the other state without a visa for 90 days from the date of entry. Agreement enters into force 90 days after both parties complete domestic procedures and notify each other through diplomatic channels. Entry into force date not independently confirmed from a DRC official source.

gaps: Maximum stay duration for Tanzania and Uganda visa-free entry is not specified in any official DRC government source; only the Kenya reciprocal (30 days) is confirmed from the Kenya-side of the agreement.

Implementation consistency for the Uganda waiver is uncertain: the EAC press release confirms the agreement was made, but some travel aggregators still report Ugandans require a visa for DRC entry, suggesting the DRC-side waiver may not yet be fully implemented or enforced.
The official DGM website (dgm.cd) does not publish a public navigable visa policy section with nationality-by-nationality breakdowns.
No official Ministry of Foreign Affairs website for DRC (minaffet.gouv.cd or diplomatie.gouv.cd) was found to be publicly accessible with visa policy content.
No official information found on citizenship-by-investment, golden visa, or investor residence programs.
No official information found on digital nomad, startup, or fast-track skilled-worker visa schemes.

No official DRC government source confirms visa-free or visa-on-arrival entry for holders of third-country credentials (US visa, Schengen visa, UK visa, Canadian visa, resident permits, permanent residence, OCI cards). Multiple aggregator sites claim such rules exist, but these could not be confirmed on any official DRC source.

The Russia-DRC agreement (mid.ru, signed 27 July 2023) specifies the agreement enters force 90 days after both parties complete domestic procedures. Entry into force date has not been independently confirmed from a DRC official source.

Other diplomatic/service passport bilateral agreements (Angola, South Africa, UAE, Iran, Turkey) were mentioned in non-official sources but could not be confirmed from official DRC or counterpart government sources.
DRC's COMESA Protocol on Free Movement of Persons ratification/implementation status not confirmed from official sources.
SADC Protocol on Facilitation of Movement of Persons — DRC implementation status not confirmed from official sources.
Status of Burundi and Rwanda as potentially visa-free based on EAC membership not confirmed from any official DRC source.
validation_notes: ADDED: Kenya (KEN) to visa_free with 30-day max stay, effective September 1, 2023 — confirmed via EAC official press release (eac.int) citing DGM announcement.
ADDED: Tanzania (TZA) to visa_free, effective March 20, 2025 — confirmed via Tanzania MoFA announcement reported in The Citizen; max stay not officially confirmed.

ADDED: Uganda (UGA) to visa_free — EAC official press release confirms reciprocal waiver agreed October 21, 2023; implementation date for Uganda admitting DRC nationals was January 1, 2024; DRC-side waiver included in same agreement but implementation on ground is uncertain.
ADDED: EAC membership to agreements list (DRC joined July 2022), as EAC is the legal basis for the Kenya/Tanzania/Uganda waivers.
UPDATED: data_quality.completeness from 'low' to 'medium' reflecting the newly confirmed EAC visa-free entries.
RETAINED: Russia conditional_access entry (diplomatic/service passports, 90 days) — source mid.ru still cited and valid.

RETAINED: e-visa entry unchanged — evisa.gouv.cd was not accessible for live re-verification but all available secondary and official sources confirm the same fee structure (USD 300 + USD 90 VAP, 7-day VAP, 72-hour processing).
RETAINED: cbi has_program=false — no new official source found for any CBI program.
RETAINED: rbi Establishment Visa — ambadrcusa.org confirmed this is still current.
NO CHANGE: visa_on_arrival remains empty — no official DRC source confirms any nationality-specific VOA scheme.

outbound_official.note: no official Democratic Republic of the Congo government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data

sources_checked.notes: Official DRC e-visa portal operated by Direction Générale de Migration (.gouv.cd domain). Page returned empty content on re-fetch — likely JavaScript-rendered; previously confirmed content treated as accurate.

Official FAQ: e-visa types, costs (USD 300 total), processing (72 hrs), VAP (7 days, USD 90), 3-month validity. Page returned empty on re-fetch (JS-rendered); prior session content confirmed consistent with secondary sources.
Official Direction Générale de Migration website; confirms DGM mandate and references evisa.gouv.cd portal; no public visa exemption list found; confirms DRC is open to entrepreneurs and tourists.

Official DRC Embassy in Washington DC; visa fees confirmed (transit USD 45/75, single entry USD 100–400, multiple entry USD 120–450); US-DRC reciprocity agreement (USD 185, 2-year visa for US citizens); non-US citizens applying via embassy must present Permanent Resident Card, US visa, and I-94 (this is an embassy application requirement, not a conditional entry rule).

Official EAC press release confirming Kenya (effective August 25, 2023) and Tanzania (effective September 1, 2023) waived visa requirements for DRC nationals, and DRC reciprocated by lifting visa restrictions for Kenyan nationals effective September 1, 2023.

Official EAC press release confirming Uganda and DRC agreed to mutually waive visa requirements during 8th JPC on October 21, 2023 in Kinshasa. Uganda became 3rd EAC state to waive for DRC. Reciprocal — both countries included.

Official Russian Ministry of Foreign Affairs press release dated 28 July 2023: confirms Agreement between Russia and DRC on visa-free travel for diplomatic and service passport holders, signed 27 July 2023 in St. Petersburg. 90-day stay.
SADC official member states page confirming DRC membership in SADC.
COMESA official member states page confirming DRC (listed as D.R Congo) is a COMESA member.
